title: PM Modi's June 2026 Europe Visit: France, Slovakia and G7 Participation Confirmed
lede: India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit France and Slovakia from June 14–18, 2026, including participation in the G7 Summit, according to an official MEA briefing on June 12, 2026.

sections.context: The Ministry of External Affairs held a special briefing on June 12, 2026, to outline Prime Minister Modi's upcoming European tour spanning June 14–18, 2026. The visit covers three destinations in France and includes participation in the G7 Summit. The briefing was led by Secretary (West) Sibi George, alongside Additional Secretaries Pooja Kapoor (North Europe) and Piyush Srivastava (Europe West), and Joint Secretary Shambhu Hakki (Multi Economic Relations). This high-level coordination across multiple MEA divisions signals the visit's significance across bilateral, multilateral, and economic dimensions. The timing reflects India's continued engagement with major Western democracies and its role in global economic governance frameworks.
sections.threads: PM Modi's European engagement aligns with India's broader strategic positioning within multilateral forums. [[india-fr-modis-june-2026-europe-tour-france-elevates-india-slova-2026|Modi's June 2026 Europe Tour]] marks another chapter in India's deepening ties with France, a long-standing strategic partner. G7 participation underscores India's voice in shaping global policy despite its formal non-membership status. The inclusion of Slovakia in the itinerary reflects India's engagement with Central European nations and diversification of bilateral partnerships beyond traditional Western capitals.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor the outcomes of Modi's bilateral meetings in France and Slovakia, particularly regarding economic cooperation, defence partnerships, and India's positioning on G7 consensus issues. The visit's framing as 'highly productive and wide-ranging' suggests multiple tracks of engagement will be pursued. Watch for joint statements or communiqués issued post-visit that may outline new commitments in trade, technology, or strategic cooperation. The involvement of the Multi Economic Relations Joint Secretary hints at economic and trade dimensions that may be elevated during the visit.
